text: There is a pervasive quietness here, broken only by the low hum of distant lights and the faint echo of my footsteps in the corridors. The dim, institutional lighting and the emptiness of these spaces heighten my sense of solitude. I feel like a witness to stillness, as if the world is paused and I am supposed to reflect deeply on the structures—physical and conceptual—surrounding me. I am at once unsettled by the silence and comforted by the opportunity to contemplate unnoticed.
